volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hippolytus (Section 1 - Section 2.150)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Πολλὴ μὲν ἐν βροτοῖσι κοὐκ ἀνώνυμος ,
sections.translation: Wide o ’ er man my realm extends , and proud the name that I , the goddess Cypris , bear , both in heaven ’ s courts and ’ mongst all those who dwell within the limits of the sea [*] and the bounds of Atlas , beholding the sun-god ’ s light ;

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: τοὺς μὲν σέβοντας τἀμὰ πρεσβεύω κράτη ,
sections.translation: those that respect my power I advance to honour , but bring to ruin all who vaunt themselves at me . For even in the race of gods this feeling finds a home , even pleasure at the honour men pay them .

sections.label: Section 9
sections.original: δείξω δὲ μύθων τῶνδ ’ ἀλήθειαν τάχα ·
sections.translation: And the truth of this I soon will show ;

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ὁ γάρ με Θησέως παῖς , Ἀμαζόνος τόκος ,
sections.translation: for that son of Theseus , born of the Amazon , Hippolytus , whom holy Pittheus taught , alone of all the dwellers in this land of Troezen , calls me vilest of the deities . Love he scorns , and , as for marriage , will none of it ;

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: Φοίβου δ ’ ἀδελφὴν Ἄρτεμιν , Διὸς κόρην ,
sections.translation: but Artemis , daughter of Zeus , sister of Phoebus , he doth honour , counting her the chief of goddesses , and ever through the greenwood , attendant on his virgin goddess , he dears the earth of wild beasts with his fleet hounds , enjoying the comradeship of one too high for mortal ken .

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: τούτοισι μέν νυν οὐ φθονῶ · τί γάρ με δεῖ ;
sections.translation: ’ Tis not this I grudge him , no ! why should I ? But for his sins against me , I will this very day take vengeance on Hippolytus ; for long ago I cleared the ground of many obstacles , so it needs but trifling toil .

sections.label: Section 24
sections.original: ἐλθόντα γάρ νιν Πιτθέως ποτ ’ ἐκ δόμων
sections.translation: For as he came one day from the home of Pittheus

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: σεμνῶν ἐς ὄψιν καὶ τέλη μυστηρίων
sections.translation: to witness the solemn mystic rites and be initiated therein in Pandion ’ s land , [*] Phaedra , his father ’ s noble wife , caught sight of him , and by my designs she found her heart was seized with wild desire .

sections.label: Section 29
sections.original: καὶ πρὶν μὲν ἐλθεῖν τήνδε γῆν Τροζηνίαν ,
sections.translation: And ere she came to this Troezenian realm ,

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: πέτραν παρ ’ αὐτὴν Παλλάδος , κατόψιον
sections.translation: a temple did she rear to Cypris hard by the rock of Pallas where it o ’ erlooks this country , for love of the youth in another land ; and to win his love in days to come she called after his name the temple she had founded for the goddess .

sections.label: Section 34
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δὲ Θησεὺς Κεκροπίαν λείπει χθόνα ,
sections.translation: Now , when Theseus left the land of Cecrops ,

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: μίασμα φεύγων αἵματος Παλλαντιδῶν ,
sections.translation: flying the pollution of the blood of Pallas ’ [*] sons , and with his wife sailed to this shore , content to suffer exile for a year , then began the wretched wife to pine away in silence , moaning ’ neath love ’ s cruel scourge ,

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: σιγῇ · ξύνοιδε δ ’ οὔτις οἰκετῶν νόσον .
sections.translation: and none of her servants knows what ails her . But this passion of hers must not fail thus . No , I will discover the matter to Theseus , and all shall be laid bare . Then will the father slay his child , my bitter foe , by curses ,

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: ἄναξ Ποσειδῶν ὤπασεν Θησεῖ γέρας ,
sections.translation: for the lord Poseidon granted this boon to Theseus ; three wishes of the god to ask , nor ever ask in vain . So Phaedra is to die , an honoured death ’ tis true , but still to die ; for I will not let her suffering outweigh the payment of such forfeit by my foes

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: δίκην τοσαύτην ὥστ ’ ἐμοὶ καλῶς ἔχειν .
sections.translation: as shall satisfy my honour .

sections.label: Section 51
sections.original: ἀλλ ’, εἰσορῶ γὰρ τόνδε παῖδα Θησέως
sections.translation: But lo ! I see the son of Theseus coming hither — Hippolytus , fresh from the labours of the chase . I will get me hence . At his back follows a long train

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: κῶμος λέλακεν , Ἄρτεμιν τιμῶν θεὰν
sections.translation: of retainers , in joyous cries of revelry uniting and hymns of praise to Artemis , his goddess ; for little he recks that Death hath oped his gates for him , and that this is his last look upon the light .

sections.label: Section 58
sections.original: ἕπεσθ ’ ἀείδοντες ἕπεσθε
sections.translation: Come follow , friends , singing to Artemis , daughter of Zeus , throned in the sky ,

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: Ἄρτεμιν , ᾇ μελόμεσθα .
sections.translation: whose votaries we are .

sections.label: Section 61
sections.original: πότνια πότνια σεμνοτάτα ,
sections.translation: Lady goddess , awful queen , daughter of Zeus , all hail ! hail ! child of

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: Λατοῦς Ἄρτεμι καὶ Διός ,
sections.translation: Latona and of Zeus , peerless mid the virgin choir , who hast thy dwelling in heaven ’ s wide mansions at thy noble father ’ s court , in the golden house of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: χαῖρέ μοι , ὦ καλλίστα καλ-
sections.translation: All hail ! most beauteous Artemis , lovelier far than all the daughters of Olympus ! [*]

sections.label: Section 73
sections.original: σοὶ τόνδε πλεκτὸν στέφανον ἐξ ἀκηράτου
sections.translation: For [*] thee , O mistress mine , I bring this woven wreath , culled from a virgin meadow ,

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: ἔνθ ’ οὔτε ποιμὴν ἀξιοῖ φέρβειν βοτὰ
sections.translation: where nor shepherd dares to herd his flock nor ever scythe hath mown , but o ’ er the mead unshorn the bee doth wing its way in spring ; and with the dew from rivers drawn purity that garden tends . Such as know no cunning lore , yet in whose nature

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: τὸ σωφρονεῖν εἴληχεν ἐς τὰ πάνθ ’ ὁμῶς ,
sections.translation: self-control , made perfect , hath a home , these may pluck the flowers , but not the wicked world . Accept , I pray , dear mistress , mine this chaplet from my holy hand to crown thy locks of gold ; for I , and none other of mortals , have this high guerdon ,

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: σοὶ καὶ ξύνειμι καὶ λόγοις ἀμείβομαι ,
sections.translation: to be with thee , with thee converse , hearing thy voice , though not thy face beholding . So be it mine to end my life as I began .

sections.label: Section 88
sections.original: ἄναξ — θεοὺς γὰρ δεσπότας καλεῖν χρεών —
sections.translation: My prince ! we needs must call upon the gods , our lords , so wilt thou listen to a friendly word from me ?

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: καὶ κάρτα γ ’· ἦ γὰρ οὐ σοφοὶ φαινοίμεθ ’ ἄν .
sections.translation: Why , that will I ! else were I proved a fool .

sections.label: Section 91
sections.original: οἶσθ ’ οὖν βροτοῖσιν ὃς καθέστηκεν νόμος ⋯
sections.translation: Dost know , then , the way of the world ?

sections.label: Section 92
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα · τοῦ δὲ καί μ ’ ἀνιστορεῖς πέρι ;
sections.translation: Not I ; but wherefore such a question ?

sections.label: Section 93
sections.original: μισεῖν τὸ σεμνὸν καὶ τὸ μὴ πᾶσιν φίλον .
sections.translation: It hates reserve which careth not for all men ’ s love .

sections.label: Section 94
sections.original: ὀρθῶς γε · τίς δ ’ οὐ σεμνὸς ἀχθεινὸς βροτῶν ;
sections.translation: And rightly too ; reserve in man is ever galling .

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: ἐν δ ’ εὐπροσηγόροισιν ἔστι τις χάρις ;
sections.translation: But there ’ s a charm in courteous affability ?

sections.label: Section 96
sections.original: πλείστη γε , καὶ κέρδος γε σὺν μόχθῳ βραχεῖ .
sections.translation: The greatest surely ; aye , and profit , too , at trifling cost .

sections.label: Section 97
sections.original: ἦ κἀν θεοῖσι ταὐτὸν ἐλπίζεις τόδε ;
sections.translation: Dost think the same law holds in heaven as well ?

sections.label: Section 98
sections.original: εἴπερ γε θνητοὶ θεῶν νόμοισι χρώμεθα .
sections.translation: I trow it doth , since all our laws we men from heaven draw .

sections.label: Section 99
sections.original: πῶς οὖν σὺ σεμνός , δαίμον ’ οὐ προσεννέπων ⋯
sections.translation: Why , then , dost thou neglect to greet an august goddess ? [*]

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: τίν ’; εὐλαβοῦ δὲ μή τί σου σφαλῇ στόμα .
sections.translation: Whom speak ’ st thou of ? Keep watch upon thy tongue lest it some mischief cause .

sections.label: Section 101
sections.original: τήνδ ’, ἣ πύλαισι σαῖς ἐφέστηκεν Κύπρις .
sections.translation: Cypris I mean , whose image is stationed o ’ er thy gate .

sections.label: Section 102
sections.original: πρόσωθεν αὐτὴν ἁγνὸς ὢν ἀσπάζομαι .
sections.translation: I greet her from afar , preserving still my chastity .

sections.label: Section 103
sections.original: σεμνός γε μέντοι κἀπίσημος ἐν βροτοῖς .
sections.translation: Yet is she an august goddess , far renowned on earth .

sections.label: Section 104
sections.original: ἄλλοισιν ἄλλος θεῶν τε κἀνθρώπων μέλει .
sections.translation: ’ Mongst gods as well as men we have our several preferences .

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: εὐδαιμονοίης νοῦν ἔχων ὅσον σε δεῖ .
sections.translation: I wish thee luck , and wisdom too , so far as thou dost need it .

sections.label: Section 106
sections.original: οὐδείς μ ’ ἀρέσκει νυκτὶ θαυμαστὸς θεῶν .
sections.translation: No god , whose worship craves the night , hath charms for me .

sections.label: Section 107
sections.original: τιμαῖσιν , ὦ παῖ , δαιμόνων χρῆσθαι χρεών .
sections.translation: My son , we should avail us of the gifts that gods confer .

sections.label: Section 108
sections.original: χωρεῖτ ’, ὀπαδοί , καὶ παρελθόντες δόμους
sections.translation: Go in , my faithful followers , and make ready food within the house ; a well-filled board hath charms after the chase is o ’ er .

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: τράπεζα πλήρης · καὶ καταψήχειν χρεὼν
sections.translation: Rub down my steeds ye must , that when I have had my all I may yoke them to the chariot and give them proper exercise .

sections.label: Section 113
sections.original: τὴν σὴν δὲ Κύπριν πόλλ ’ ἐγὼ χαίρειν λέγω .
sections.translation: As for thy Queen of Love , a long farewell to her .

sections.label: Section 114
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δέ — τοὺς νέους γὰρ οὐ μιμητέον ,
sections.translation: Meantime I with sober mind , for I must not copy my young master ,

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: φρονοῦντας οὕτως — ὡς πρέπει δούλοις λέγειν ,
sections.translation: do offer up my prayer to thy image , lady Cypris , in such words as it becomes a slave to use . But thou should ’ st pardon all , who , in youth ’ s impetuous heat , speak idle words of thee ; make as though thou hearest not ,

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: σοφωτέρους γὰρ χρὴ βροτῶν εἶναι θεούς .
sections.translation: for gods must needs be wiser than the sons of men .

sections.label: Section 1.121
sections.original: Ὠκεανοῦ τις ὕδωρ στάζουσα πέτρα λέγεται ,
sections.translation: A rock there is , where , as they say , the ocean dew distils , and from its beetling brow it pours a copious stream for pitchers to be dipped therein ;

sections.label: Section 1.125
sections.original: ὅθι μοί τις ἦν φίλα
sections.translation: ’ twas here I had a friend washing robes of purple in the trickling stream , and she was spreading them out on the face of a warm sunny rock ; from her

sections.label: Section 1.130
sections.original: μοι πρώτα φάτις ἦλθε δεσποίνας ·
sections.translation: I had the tidings , first of all , that my mistress

sections.label: Section 1.131
sections.original: τειρομέναν νοσερᾷ κοίτᾳ δέμας ἐντὸς ἔχειν
sections.translation: she was wasting on the bed of sickness , pent within her house , a thin veil o ’ ershadowing her head of golden hair .

sections.label: Section 1.135
sections.original: τριτάταν δέ νιν κλύω
sections.translation: And this is the third day I hear that she hath closed her lovely lips and denied her chaste body all sustenance , eager to hide her suffering

sections.label: Section 1.140
sections.original: σαν κέλσαι ποτὶ τέρμα δύστανον .
sections.translation: and reach death ’ s cheerless bourn .

sections.label: Section 2.141
sections.original: — ἦ σὺ γ ’ ἔνθεος , ὦ κούρα ,
sections.translation: Maiden , thou must be possessed , by Pan made frantic or by Hecate , or by the Corybantes dread , and Cybele the mountain mother .

sections.label: Section 2.145
sections.original: — σὺ δ ’ ἀμφὶ τὰν πολύθηρον Δί-
sections.translation: Or maybe thou hast sinned against Dictynna , huntress-queen , and art wasting for thy guilt in sacrifice unoffered . For she doth range o ’ er lakes ’ expanse and past the bounds of earth

sections.label: Section 2.150
sections.original: δίναις ἐν νοτίαις ἅλμας .
sections.translation: upon the ocean ’ s tossing billows .
